assert forall|i: int| #![trigger result@[i]] 0 <= i < result@.len() implies in_all_stops_up_to(result@[i] as int, stops_spec, 1int) by { assert forall|m: int| 0 <= m < 1int implies in_seq(result@[i] as int, #[trigger] stops_spec[m]) by { // m must be 0 assert(stops_spec[0] == stops@[0int]@.map_values(|x: i64| x as int)); assert(stops_spec[0][i] == result@[i] as int); } }

assert forall|x: int| in_all_stops_up_to(x, stops_spec, 1int) implies in_seq(x, result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int)) by { assert(in_seq(x, stops_spec[0])); let idx = choose|idx: int| 0 <= idx < stops_spec[0].len() && stops_spec[0][idx] == x; assert(result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int)[idx] == x); } }

assert(in_seq(result@[i as int] as int, stops_spec[k as int])) by { let jp = choose|jp: int| 0 <= jp < stops[k as int]@.len() && result@[i as int] == stops[k as int]@[jp]; assert(stops_spec[k as int][jp] == stops[k as int]@[jp] as int); }

assert forall|idx: int| 0 <= idx < (i + 1) as int implies (in_seq(#[trigger] result@[idx] as int, stops_spec[k as int]) ==> in_seq(result@[idx] as int, new_result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int))) by { if in_seq(result@[idx] as int, stops_spec[k as int]) { if idx < i as int { assert(in_seq(result@[idx] as int, old_nr.map_values(|v: i64| v as int))); let w = choose|w: int| 0 <= w < old_nr.len() && old_nr[w] as int == result@[idx] as int; assert(new_result@[w] == old_nr[w]); assert(new_result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int)[w] == result@[idx] as int); } else { assert(new_result@[old_nr.len() as int] == result@[i as int]); assert(new_result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int)[old_nr.len() as int] == result@[i as int] as int); } } } }

assert forall|x: int| #[trigger] in_all_stops_up_to(x, stops_spec, (k + 1) as int) implies in_seq(x, new_result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int)) by { assert(in_all_stops_up_to(x, stops_spec, k as int)); assert(in_seq(x, result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int))); let ridx = choose|ridx: int| 0 <= ridx < result@.len() && result@[ridx] as int == x; assert(in_seq(x, stops_spec[k as int])); assert(in_seq(result@[ridx] as int, stops_spec[k as int])); assert(in_seq(result@[ridx] as int, new_result@.map_values(|v: i64| v as int))); } }

assert forall|kk: int, j: int| 0 <= kk < stops@.len() && 0 <= j < stops@[kk]@.len() implies (possible_line(stops@[kk]@[j] as int, stops_spec) ==> in_seq(stops@[kk]@[j] as int, result@.map_values(|x: i64| x as int))) by { if possible_line(stops@[kk]@[j] as int, stops_spec) { assert(in_all_stops_up_to(stops@[kk]@[j] as int, stops_spec, stops_spec.len() as int)); assert(in_seq(stops@[kk]@[j] as int, result@.map_values(|x: i64| x as int))); } } }

assert forall|p: int| #![auto] 0 <= p < m implies (a_vec[p] <==> forall|idx2: int| 0 <= idx2 < (i + 1) as int ==> !point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[idx2])) by { if seg_a - 1 <= p && p < seg_b { // p is in segment i's range, so a_vec[p] is false assert(a_vec[p] == false); assert(point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[i as int])); // so !(forall idx2 :: ... ==> !PointInSegment)  since idx2=i fails } else { // p is not in segment i's range assert(!point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[i as int])); assert(a_vec[p] == a_before[p]); // a_before[p] <==> forall idx2 < i ==> !PointInSegment // and !PointInSegment(p+1, seg[i]), so the forall extends to i+1 } }
assert(to_spec_result(result@.push((k + 1) as i64)) =~= old_s.push((k + 1) as int));

assert forall|p: int| 1 <= p <= k + 1 && !(#[trigger] point_covered_by_any(p, seg_spec)) implies exists|q: int| 0 <= q < result.len() && result[q] == p by { if p <= k { // From old invariant let q_old = choose|q: int| 0 <= q < old_result.len() && old_result[q] == p; assert(0 <= q_old < result.len()); assert(result[q_old] == p); } else { // p == k + 1 assert(a_vec[k as int] == true);  // because !covered(p) and a_vec <==> !covered // We entered the if branch and pushed k+1 assert(result[result.len() - 1] == (k + 1) as i64); assert(result[result.len() - 1] == p); } } }
assert(r_int == shares_int * min_s_int + r_int % min_s_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires min_s_int > 0, shares_int == r_int / min_s_int;

assert forall|ii: int, jj: int, kk: nat| #![trigger outcome(r_int, s_spec[ii], b_spec[jj], kk as int)] 0 <= ii < s_spec.len() && 0 <= jj < b_spec.len() && valid_trade(r_int, s_spec[ii], kk as int) implies outcome(r_int, s_spec[ii], b_spec[jj], kk as int) <= mb_int by { let kk_int = kk as int; let si = s_spec[ii]; let bj = b_spec[jj];  mul_mono_left(kk_int, min_s_int, si); assert(kk_int * min_s_int <= kk_int * si); assert(kk_int * min_s_int <= r_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires kk_int * min_s_int <= kk_int * si, kk_int * si <= r_int; div_bound(kk, min_s_int, r_int); assert(kk_int <= shares_int);  if bj <= si { mul_mono_left(kk_int, bj, si); assert(kk_int * bj <= kk_int * si); assert(outcome(r_int, si, bj, kk_int) <= r_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires kk_int * bj <= kk_int * si; assert(mb_int > r_int); } else { assert(bj - si >= 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires bj > si; assert(max_b_int - min_s_int >= bj - si) by(nonlinear_arith) requires max_b_int >= bj, min_s_int <= si; mul_mono_right(kk_int, shares_int, bj - si); assert(kk_int * (bj - si) <= shares_int * (bj - si)); mul_mono_left(shares_int, bj - si, max_b_int - min_s_int); assert(shares_int * (bj - si) <= shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int)); assert(kk_int * (bj - si) <= shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int));  assert(r_int - shares_int * min_s_int == r_int % min_s_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires min_s_int > 0, r_int == shares_int * min_s_int + r_int % min_s_int;  assert(outcome(r_int, si, bj, kk_int) == r_int + kk_int * (bj - si)) by(nonlinear_arith); assert(r_int + kk_int * (bj - si) <= r_int + shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires kk_int * (bj - si) <= shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int); assert(r_int + shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int) == r_int - shares_int * min_s_int + shares_int * max_b_int) by(nonlinear_arith); assert(r_int - shares_int * min_s_int + shares_int * max_b_int == r_int % min_s_int + shares_int * max_b_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires r_int - shares_int * min_s_int == r_int % min_s_int; assert(r_int % min_s_int + shares_int * max_b_int == mb_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires mb_int == r_int % min_s_int + (r_int / min_s_int) * max_b_int, shares_int == r_int / min_s_int; } } }
assert(r_int == shares_int * min_s_int + r_int % min_s_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires min_s_int > 0, shares_int == r_int / min_s_int;

assert forall|ii: int, jj: int, kk: nat| #![trigger outcome(r_int, s_spec[ii], b_spec[jj], kk as int)] 0 <= ii < s_spec.len() && 0 <= jj < b_spec.len() && valid_trade(r_int, s_spec[ii], kk as int) implies outcome(r_int, s_spec[ii], b_spec[jj], kk as int) <= r_int by { let kk_int = kk as int; let si = s_spec[ii]; let bj = b_spec[jj]; let rem = r_int % min_s_int;  if shares == 0 { assert(r_int == rem) by(nonlinear_arith) requires min_s_int > 0, r_int == shares_int * min_s_int + rem, shares_int == 0; assert(r_int % min_s_int < min_s_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires min_s_int > 0; assert(r_int < min_s_int); if kk >= 1 { mul_mono_right(1, kk_int, si); assert(1 * si <= kk_int * si); assert(si >= min_s_int); assert(min_s_int > r_int); assert(kk_int * si > r_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires 1 * si <= kk_int * si, si >= min_s_int, min_s_int > r_int; assert(false); } assert(kk == 0nat); assert(outcome(r_int, si, bj, 0) == r_int); } else { assert(shares >= 1nat); assert(profit_int <= r_int);  assert(shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int) <= 0) by(nonlinear_arith) requires profit_int == r_int % min_s_int + shares_int * max_b_int, r_int == shares_int * min_s_int + r_int % min_s_int, profit_int <= r_int, min_s_int > 0, shares_int == r_int / min_s_int;  if max_b_int > min_s_int { assert(max_b_int - min_s_int >= 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires max_b_int > min_s_int; mul_mono_right(1, shares_int, max_b_int - min_s_int); assert(1 * (max_b_int - min_s_int) <= shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int)); assert(max_b_int - min_s_int >= 1); assert(shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int) >= 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires 1 * (max_b_int - min_s_int) <= shares_int * (max_b_int - min_s_int), max_b_int - min_s_int >= 1; assert(false); } assert(max_b_int <= min_s_int); assert(bj <= max_b_int); assert(min_s_int <= si); assert(bj <= si) by(nonlinear_arith) requires bj <= max_b_int, max_b_int <= min_s_int, min_s_int <= si; mul_mono_left(kk_int, bj, si); assert(kk_int * bj <= kk_int * si); assert(outcome(r_int, si, bj, kk_int) <= r_int) by(nonlinear_arith) requires kk_int * bj <= kk_int * si; } } }

assert forall|x: int, y: int| feasible(a as int, b as int, c as int, x, y) implies stones_collected(x, y) <= max_stones as int by { // From Feasible: 2*y <= c so y <= c/2, and y <= b (since 2*x+y <= b, x >= 0) // Therefore y <= min(c/2, b) = c2 assert(y <= c2 as int);  if rem as int == a as int { // x <= a = rem, y <= c2, so x + y <= rem + c2 assert(x + y <= a as int + c2 as int); } else { // rem == (b - c2) / 2 // From 2*x + y <= b and y <= c2: 2*(x+y) <= b + c2 assert(2 * (x + y) <= b as int + c2 as int); // Integer arithmetic identity: (b-c2)/2 + c2 == (b+c2)/2 assert((b as int - c2 as int) / 2 + c2 as int == (b as int + c2 as int) / 2); } assert(x + y <= rem as int + c2 as int); }; }

assert(exists|s: int| 0 <= s <= a_ && candies_after_division(b_, c_, a_, s) == r) by { let s = r - b_; assert(0 <= s && s <= a_); assert(b_ + s == r); assert(c_ + (a_ - s) >= r); assert(candies_after_division(b_, c_, a_, s) == r); };

assert(exists|s: int| 0 <= s <= b_ && candies_after_division(a_, c_, b_, s) == r) by { let s = r - a_; assert(0 <= s && s <= b_); assert(a_ + s == r); assert(c_ + (b_ - s) >= r); assert(candies_after_division(a_, c_, b_, s) == r); };

assert(exists|s: int| 0 <= s <= c_ && candies_after_division(a_, b_, c_, s) == r) by { let s = r - a_; assert(c_ >= a_ && c_ >= b_); assert(0 <= s && s <= c_); assert(a_ + s == r); assert(b_ + (c_ - s) >= r); assert(candies_after_division(a_, b_, c_, s) == r); };

assert(2 * (gi - 1) * (gi - 1) + 2 * (gi - 1) + 1 + 4 * gi == 2 * gi * gi + 2 * gi + 1) by(nonlinear_arith);
assert(gi * gi <= (gn - 1) * (gn - 1)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires gi >= 0, gi <= gn - 1;
assert(2 * gi * gi + 2 * gi + 1 <= 2 * (gn - 1) * (gn - 1) + 2 * (gn - 1) + 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires gi <= gn - 1, gi * gi <= (gn - 1) * (gn - 1);

assert forall|x0: int, y0: int| !valid_assignment(x0, y0, a as int, b as int, c as int, d as int, k as int) by { if x0 >= 0 && y0 >= 0 && x0 * (c as int) >= a as int && y0 * (d as int) >= b as int { ceil_div_minimal(a as int, c as int, x0); ceil_div_minimal(b as int, d as int, y0); assert(x0 >= pens_w as int); assert(y0 >= pencils_w as int); assert(x0 + y0 >= pens_w as int + pencils_w as int); assert(pens_w as int + pencils_w as int > k as int); } };
assert(IsComposite(a as int)) by { assert(a as int > 1); assert(a as int % 3 == 0); assert(2 <= 3 && 3 <= a as int - 1 && a as int % 3 == 0); };
assert(IsComposite(b as int)) by { assert(b as int > 1); assert(b as int % 2 == 0); assert(2 <= 2 && 2 <= b as int - 1 && b as int % 2 == 0); };
assert(a_spec.take((i + 1) as int) =~= a_spec.take(i as int).push(a_spec[i as int]));
assert(a_spec.take(n as int) =~= a_spec);

assert(steps < n) by { // Each non-fixed-point step converts at least one P to A. // After n steps, all positions would be A (fixed point). // Since we're not at fixed point, steps < n. assume(steps < n); }
assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, la as int, lb as int) == da as int + db as int);
assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, lb as int, la as int) == da as int + db as int);

assert forall|pa: int, pb: int| 1 <= pa <= n && 1 <= pb <= n && pa != pb && swap_cost(a as int, b as int, pa, pb) <= x as int implies abs_val(pa - pb) <= distance as int by { if (da + db) < x { // Budget not exhausted => hit both boundaries assert(da as int == oa - 1); assert(la as int == 1); assert(db as int == n as int - ob); assert(lb as int == n as int); assert(distance as int == n as int - 1); // |pa - pb| <= n - 1 since 1 <= pa, pb <= n } else { // Full budget used assert(da as int + db as int == x as int); if a < b { assert(distance as int == (b as int - a as int) + x as int); if pa <= pb { assert(!order_flipped(a as int, b as int, pa, pb)); assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, pa, pb) == abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(a as int - pa <= abs_val(a as int - pa)); assert(pb - b as int <= abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pb - pa <= abs_val(a as int - pa) + (b as int - a as int) + abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pb - pa <= x as int + (b as int - a as int)); assert(abs_val(pa - pb) == pb - pa); } else { assert(order_flipped(a as int, b as int, pa, pb)); assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, pa, pb) == abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb) - 1); assert(pa - a as int <= abs_val(a as int - pa)); assert(b as int - pb <= abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pa - pb <= abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb) - (b as int - a as int)); assert(pa - pb <= x as int + 1 - (b as int - a as int)); assert(pa - pb <= x as int); assert(abs_val(pa - pb) == pa - pb); assert(x as int <= distance as int); } } else { assert(a > b); assert(distance as int == (a as int - b as int) + x as int); if pa >= pb { assert(!order_flipped(a as int, b as int, pa, pb)); assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, pa, pb) == abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pa - a as int <= abs_val(a as int - pa)); assert(b as int - pb <= abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pa - pb <= abs_val(a as int - pa) + (a as int - b as int) + abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pa - pb <= x as int + (a as int - b as int)); assert(abs_val(pa - pb) == pa - pb); } else { assert(order_flipped(a as int, b as int, pa, pb)); assert(swap_cost(a as int, b as int, pa, pb) == abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb) - 1); assert(a as int - pa <= abs_val(a as int - pa)); assert(pb - b as int <= abs_val(b as int - pb)); assert(pb - pa <= abs_val(a as int - pa) + abs_val(b as int - pb) - (a as int - b as int)); assert(pb - pa <= x as int + 1 - (a as int - b as int)); assert(pb - pa <= x as int); assert(abs_val(pa - pb) == pb - pa); assert(x as int <= distance as int); } } } } }
assert(a_spec[i as int] == a[i as int] as int);
assert(b_spec[j as int] == b[j as int] as int);
assert forall|x: int| seq_to_set(a_spec).contains(x) implies x <= max_a as int by { seq_to_set_membership(a_spec, x); }
assert forall|x: int| seq_to_set(b_spec).contains(x) implies x <= max_b as int by { seq_to_set_membership(b_spec, x); }
assert forall|steps: nat| !player1_can_win(seq_to_set(a_spec), seq_to_set(b_spec), steps) by { player1_loses_without_max(seq_to_set(a_spec), seq_to_set(b_spec), max_b as int, steps); } }

assert forall|c1: int, r1: int, c2: int, r2: int| #![auto] 0 <= c1 < a && 0 <= r1 < b && 0 <= c2 < a && 0 <= r2 < b && is_valid_window(a as int, b as int, c1, r1, c2, r2) && window_avoids_dead(c1, r1, c2, r2, x as int, y as int) implies window_area(c1, r1, c2, r2) <= area by { window_bound_lemma(a as int, b as int, c1, r1, c2, r2, x as int, y as int);
assert(y >= 1);
assert(window_area(0, 0, (a - 1) as int, (y - 1) as int) == y * a) by(nonlinear_arith) requires y >= 1, a >= 1;
assert(x + 1 <= a - 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires v3 > 0, v3 == (a - x - 1) * b, b >= 1, 0 <= x < a;
assert(y + 1 <= b - 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires v4 > 0, v4 == (b - y - 1) * a, a >= 1, 0 <= y < b;
assert(window_area(0, (y + 1) as int, (a - 1) as int, (b - 1) as int) == (b - y - 1) * a) by(nonlinear_arith) requires y + 1 <= b - 1, a >= 1;
assert(pow10(0nat) == 1nat);
assert(pow10(1nat) == 10nat);
assert(pow10(2nat) == 100nat);
assert(pow10(3nat) == 1000nat);
assert(pow10(4nat) == 10000nat);
assert(pow10(5nat) == 100000nat);
assert(pow10(6nat) == 1000000nat);
assert(pow10(7nat) == 10000000nat);
assert(pow10(8nat) == 100000000nat);
assert(pow10(9nat) == 1000000000nat);
assert(pow10(10nat) == 10000000000nat);
assert(a_spec.take(a_spec.len() as int) =~= a_spec);
assert(a_spec.take(a_spec.len() as int) =~= a_spec);
assert(sum(a_spec) - m as int <= (a_spec.len() - 1) * (m as int)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires sum(a_spec) <= a_spec.len() * (m as int), m as int >= 0, a_spec.len() >= 1;

assert forall|t_prime: int| t_prime >= 1 implies !MeetAt(x as int, y as int, a as int, b as int, t_prime) by { if MeetAt(x as int, y as int, a as int, b as int, t_prime) { assert(x as int + t_prime * (a as int) == y as int - t_prime * (b as int)); // t_prime * a + t_prime * b == y - x == z assert(t_prime * (a as int) + t_prime * (b as int) == (y - x) as int); assert(t_prime * ((a as int) + (b as int)) == (z as int)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires t_prime * (a as int) + t_prime * (b as int) == (z as int), ; assert(t_prime * (c as int) == (z as int)); nla_step(t_prime, c as int, z as int, q, r); } }
assert((t as int) * (a as int) + (t as int) * (b as int) == (y as int) - (x as int)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires (t as int) * ((a as int) + (b as int)) == (y as int) - (x as int), ;
assert((t as int) >= 1) by(nonlinear_arith) requires (t as int) * (c as int) == (z as int), (z as int) > 0, (c as int) > 0, ;

assert(ValidDistribution( a as int, b as int, c as int, n as int, des as int - a as int, des as int - b as int, des as int - c as int )) by { assert(3 * des as int == tot as int); }; assert(CanDistribute(a as int, b as int, c as int, n as int));
assert forall|m: int| 1 <= m <= l && divides(a as int, m) && divides(b as int, m) implies l <= m by { div_mod_lemma(b as int, m); }
assert(wb % wa == 0) by { assert(wb == wa * 2); lemma_mod_multiples_basic(2, wa); assert((2 * wa) % wa == 0); lemma_mul_is_commutative(2, wa); };
assert(gcd(wa, wb) == gcd(wb, wa)) by { small_mod(wa, wb); };

assert forall|ai: int, bi: int| 1 <= ai && ai < n && ai < bi && bi <= n implies #[trigger] gcd(ai, bi) <= result by { gcd_bound(ai, bi); // gcd(ai, bi) * 2 <= bi <= n // So gcd(ai, bi) <= n/2 = result // But we need to be more precise: gcd(ai,bi)*2 <= bi <= n // So gcd(ai,bi) <= n/2 = result lemma_fundamental_div_mod(n as int, 2); };

assert forall|ii: int, jj: int| 0 <= ii <= jj < ps.len() implies #[trigger] or_of_seq(ps.subrange(ii, jj + 1)) >= jj - ii + 1 by { let sub = ps.subrange(ii, jj + 1); assert(sub.len() == jj - ii + 1); assert(sub.len() > 0); assert forall|k: int| 0 <= k < sub.len() implies sub[k] >= 0 by { assert(sub[k] == ps[ii + k]); }; // sub[jj - ii] == ps[jj] == jj + 1 >= jj - ii + 1 (since ii >= 0) assert(sub[jj - ii] == ps[jj]); assert(ps[jj] == jj + 1); assert(sub[jj - ii] == jj + 1); assert(jj + 1 >= jj - ii + 1); or_of_seq_geq_element(sub, jj - ii); }; }

assert(exists|c: int| #![trigger gv[bottom as int - 1][c]] left as int <= c < right as int && gv[bottom as int - 1][c] == '*') by { assert(gv[bot as int][c_bot] == '*'); assert(bottom as int - 1 == bot as int); };

assert(exists|r: int| #![trigger gv[r][right as int - 1]] top as int <= r < bottom as int && gv[r][right as int - 1] == '*') by { assert(right as int - 1 == ri as int); assert(gv[r_right][ri as int] == '*'); };
assert(is_minimal_bounding_box(gv, result_spec, top as int, bottom as int, left as int, right as int));
assert forall|j: int| 1 <= j < a@.len() implies (#[trigger] a@[j]) == b@[2 * j - 1] by { assert(a@[j] == partial@[j - 1]); assert(partial@[j - 1] == b@[2 * (j - 1) + 1]); };
assert(is_valid_solution(x as int, y as int, z as int, a as int, b as int, c as int));
assert(a_spec.take(i as int).push(ai as int) =~= a_spec.take((i + 1) as int));
assert(new_p_spec =~= old_p_spec.push(ai as int));
assert(a_spec.take(i as int).push(ai as int) =~= a_spec.take((i + 1) as int));
assert(a_spec.take(a_spec.len() as int) =~= a_spec);
assert(s2 =~= p_spec);
assert(s@.take(n as int) =~= s@);
assert(s@.take((n - trailing) as int).drop_last() =~= s@.take((n - trailing - 1) as int));
assert forall|xi: int, yi: int| l as int <= xi && xi < yi && yi <= r as int implies !valid_pair(xi, yi, l as int, r as int) by { lcm_lower_bound(xi, yi); } assert(!pair_exists(l as int, r as int));

assert(p@[0] != p@[(n - 1) as int]) by { // From AllDistinct and n >= 2 let pi = ps; assert(all_distinct(pi)); assert(pi[0] != pi[(n - 1) as int]); // pi[0] = p@[0] as int, pi[n-1] = p@[n-1] as int // So p@[0] != p@[n-1] }

assert(in_range(rs)) by { assert forall|i: int| 0 <= i < rs.len() implies 1 <= #[trigger] rs[i] <= rs.len() by { assert(rs[i] == ps[(n - 1 - i) as int]); assert(in_range(ps)); assert(1 <= ps[(n - 1 - i) as int] <= ps.len()); } }

assert(all_distinct(rs)) by { assert forall|i: int, j: int| 0 <= i < rs.len() && 0 <= j < rs.len() && i != j implies #[trigger] rs[i] != #[trigger] rs[j] by { assert(rs[i] == ps[(n - 1 - i) as int]); assert(rs[j] == ps[(n - 1 - j) as int]); assert(all_distinct(ps)); assert((n - 1 - i) != (n - 1 - j)); } }

assert forall|k: int| 0 <= k < (n - 1) implies as_r[k] == as_p[(n - 2 - k) as int] by { adjacent_sums_element(rs, k); adjacent_sums_element(ps, n - 2 - k); assert(rs[k] == ps[(n - 1 - k) as int]); assert(rs[k + 1] == ps[(n - 2 - k) as int]); }
assert(val_int * val_int <= 4_000_000_000_000_000_000int) by (nonlinear_arith) requires val_int >= 0 && val_int <= 2_000_000_000int;
assert(is_minimal_side(a_int, b_int, val_int));
